Genes.Causal_Chain: Natural allelic variation at Bx10-12 (O-methyltransferases) on chromosome 1 → differential methylation of DIMBOA-Glc → variation in HDMBOA-Glc accumulation across maize diversity panel (Figure 9B). This locus is the metabolite QTL hotspot on chromosome 1 in both leaf tips and bases (Figure 8).
Genes.Effect_Direction: Different haplotypes at Bx10-12 locus are associated with differential HDMBOA-Glc accumulation; this locus is also a metabolite QTL hotspot on chromosome 1 controlling multiple benzoxazinoid mass features
Genes.Evidence_Level: Genetic association + prior functional validation (Meihls et al., 2013; Handrick et al., 2016) confirmed by GWAS in this study
Genes.Evidence_Type: GWAS mapping with 29 million SNPs confirming known biosynthetic genes; most significantly associated SNPs in LD with Bx10-12; haplotype-based additive effect analysis (Figure 9B, 9C)
Genes.Gene_ID: Chromosome 1 locus (110-kb region containing BX10, BX11, BX12 paralogs)
Genes.Metabolic_Role: Key enzyme genes; O-methyltransferases catalyzing the biosynthesis of HDMBOA-Glc from DIMBOA-Glc in the benzoxazinoid pathway
Genes.Perturbation_Type: GWAS mapping confirmed by known genetic studies; natural allelic variation across 282 maize inbred lines; haplotype analysis with biallelic haplotype inference
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: Additive effect with chromosome 9 locus on HDMBOA-Glc content (Figure 9C); tropical lines accumulate significantly more HDMBOA-Glc and less DIMBOA-Glc than temperate lines (P<0.05, ANOVA)
Genes.Tissue_Stage: Seedling leaf tips and leaf bases (third leaf stage)

Genes.Causal_Chain: Natural allelic variation at Bx13 on chromosome 2 → variation in DIM2BOA-Glc accumulation in seedling leaves (Figure 9A)
Genes.Effect_Direction: SNPs in LD with Bx13 are most strongly associated with DIM2BOA-Glc abundance variation
Genes.Evidence_Level: Genetic association confirming prior functional validation
Genes.Evidence_Type: GWAS mapping confirming known biosynthetic gene (Handrick et al., 2016); most significantly associated SNPs in LD with Bx13 (Figure 9A)
Genes.Metabolic_Role: Key enzyme gene in benzoxazinoid biosynthesis; catalyzes biosynthesis of DIM2BOA-Glc
Genes.Perturbation_Type: GWAS mapping confirmed known QTL; natural allelic variation across diversity panel
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: NA (confirmed known locus; quantitative effect shown in Manhattan plot Figure 9A)
Genes.Tissue_Stage: Seedling leaf tips and leaf bases (third leaf stage)

Genes.Causal_Chain: Novel locus on chromosome 9 containing GRMZM2G108309 (protein phosphatase 2C) → haplotypic variation affects gene expression (Figure 9D) → differential HDMBOA-Glc accumulation (Figure 9E); acts additively with Bx10-12 on chromosome 1 (Figure 9C)
Genes.Effect_Direction: Higher GRMZM2G108309 expression is associated with higher HDMBOA-Glc content (comparing top 20 vs bottom 20 expression lines); additive effect with Bx10-12 locus
Genes.Evidence_Level: Genetic association + expression-metabolite correlation (partial); weaker than direct genetic perturbation
Genes.Evidence_Type: GWAS discovery of novel locus on chromosome 9; haplotype segregation analysis; gene expression-metabolite correlation for extreme lines (Figure 9D, 9E); however, full population correlation was not significant (Supplemental Figure 5B)
Genes.Metabolic_Role: Predicted protein phosphatase 2C family protein; newly identified regulatory locus affecting HDMBOA-Glc accumulation
Genes.Perturbation_Type: GWAS mapping of natural variation; haplotype-based expression analysis; comparison of 20 highest vs 20 lowest expression lines
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: Significant difference in HDMBOA-Glc between 20 highest and 20 lowest expression lines (P<0.05, two-tailed t-test; Figure 9E); significant expression difference between haplotypes (P<0.005; Figure 9D); additive effect with chromosome 1 locus (Figure 9C)

Genes.Causal_Chain: Coding sequence variation in GRMZM2G063909 (citrate synthase-like) on chromosome 4 → altered enzyme structure → production of different structural isomers of coumaroyl, caffeoyl, and feruloyl hydroxycitric acid esters (Figures 11, 12). This locus corresponds to the metabolite QTL hotspot on chromosome 4 for both tissue types (Figure 8). Tropical maize lines carry allele producing less polar isomers; temperate lines produce more polar (2-O-acylated) isomers.
Genes.Effect_Direction: Allelic variation at GRMZM2G063909 determines which isomers of phenylpropanoid hydroxycitric acid esters accumulate: temperate allele (B73) → more polar 2-O-acylated isomers; tropical allele (Ki11, CML247) → less polar isomers. Heterozygotes show intermediate phenotype with both isomers at reduced abundance.
Genes.Evidence_Level: Genetic association + independent cosegregation validation in NILs (strong evidence)
Genes.Evidence_Type: GWAS mapping to single 10-kb LD block on chromosome 4 (Figure 11C, 11D); independent validation with cosegregation in near-isogenic lines from two RIL families (Figure 12); all linked SNPs in coding region; no expression difference between alleles (Figure 11F); NMR structural confirmation of metabolites (Figure 10C)
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: Phenylpropanoid hydroxycitric acid esters: coumaroyl hydroxycitric acid, caffeoyl hydroxycitric acid, and feruloyl hydroxycitric acid (and their structural isomers)
Genes.Intermediate_Metabolites: 2-hydroxycitric acid conjugated with coumaric acid, caffeic acid, and ferulic acid
Genes.Metabolic_Role: Citrate synthase-like enzyme; controls isomeric variation of phenylpropanoid hydroxycitric acid esters; coding sequence variation (not expression level) determines which structural isomers accumulate
Genes.Perturbation_Type: GWAS mapping; independent validation with near-isogenic lines (NILs) from B73×Ki11 and B73×CML247 RIL families segregating at this locus
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: Perfect cosegregation between genotypic markers and two classes of phenylpropanoid hydroxycitric acid esters in NILs (Supplemental Figure 7); heterozygous lines produce both isomers at lesser abundance than either homozygote (Figures 12C-12E); no significant difference in gene expression between alleles (Figure 11F), indicating structural protein variation is causal
Genes.Tissue_Stage: Seedling leaf tips and leaf bases (third leaf stage)

Genes.Causal_Chain: Higher expression of chalcone synthase genes (GRMZM2G422750, GRMZM2G380650) in leaf tips → flavonoid biosynthesis → flavonoid accumulation in leaf tips but not leaf bases (Figure 3A, 3B)
Genes.Effect_Direction: Higher chalcone synthase expression in leaf tips correlates with flavonoid presence in tips and absence in bases
Genes.Evidence_Level: Transcript changes correlated with metabolite change (weaker evidence; no direct genetic perturbation of these genes in this study)
Genes.Evidence_Type: Transcript-metabolite correlation across tissues using published transcriptomic data (Kremling et al., 2018); UV chromatogram analysis (Figure 3A, 3B)
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: Flavonoids (flavonoid-like metabolites detected in leaf tips)
Genes.Metabolic_Role: Key enzyme genes; chalcone synthases catalyzing the first committed step in flavonoid biosynthesis
Genes.Perturbation_Type: Transcriptomic comparison between leaf tips and bases across the diversity panel (no direct genetic perturbation in this study)
Genes.Quantitative_Effect: GRMZM2G422750 and GRMZM2G380650 are significantly more highly expressed in leaf tips than leaf bases (FDR<0.05, Student's t-test; Figure 3B); flavonoid UV absorption peaks completely absent in leaf base chromatograms (Figure 3A)
Genes.Tissue_Stage: Seedling leaf tips vs leaf bases (third leaf stage)

Title: Metabolome-Scale Genome-Wide Association Studies Reveal Chemical Diversity and Genetic Control of Maize Specialized Metabolites
